content.full_text:
842 Orchard Lane
Fosso

5442 M(o)
8410a

June 2, 1976

76-06 140

Mr. Allen L. Pross
Executive Secretary
California Medical Political Action Committee
731 Market Street
San Francisco CA 94101

Dear Mr. Pross:

Thank you for your letter of May 13, 1976,
concerning the Form 420 filed by the California
Medical Political Action Committee.

You indicated that the street address of the Committee
and the business address of the treasurer are included on
the Form 420. In these circumstances, the treasurer is
a professional person with an established business address,
and the staff has concluded that the treasurer is not
required to list his residential address on the Form 420.

I hope this information will be of assistance.
Please contact the staff if you have any further questions.

Sincerely,

Natalie E. West
Staff Counsel

NEW:PVP

cc: Mr. Richard W. Bryant
Office of the Registrar-Recorder
608 North Spring Street
Los Angeles California 90012

California Medical Political Action Committee    731 Market Street    San Francisco, California 94103    415/777-2000


May 18, 1976


Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, CA  95814

ATTENTION:  Natalie West

Dear Ms. West:

Pursuant to our telephone conversation, I would like to request an
opinion as to whether it is necessary under law to provide on com-
mittee campaign statements the residential address of a political
action committee's treasurer.

CALPAC has routinely filled out Form 420 indicating that the mail-
ing address for all correspondence and the phone number is as
listed above on our letterhead.  Requiring the residential address
and phone number to appear on the form smacks of invasion of privacy
and serves no useful purpose as long as the treasurer can be readily
reached at the address of the committee itself.

I know that Mr. Bryant, Elections Deputy for the County of Los Angeles,
is also interested in a solution to this problem.  If you need any
further information or desire clarification, please do not hesitate
to give me a call.


                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Allen L. Pross,
                                        Executive Secretary
ALP:jl

cc:  Mr. Richard W. Bryant


A copy of our report is filed with the Federal Election Commission and is available for purchase from the Federal Election Commission, Washington, D.C

sections.question: Pursuant to our telephone conversation, I would like to request an opinion as to whether it is necessary under law to provide on committee campaign statements the residential address of a political action committee's treasurer.
sections.question_synthetic: Does the Fair Political Practices Act require a political action committee to disclose the treasurer's residential address on campaign statements (Form 420), or is the mailing address of the committee itself and phone number sufficient?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request from CALPAC to FPPC seeking an opinion, not an FPPC response letter. No conclusion or advice from FPPC is present. Document dated May 18, 1976 (Document ID 76140). Form 420 and privacy concerns mentioned. County Elections Deputy Richard Bryant also interested in the matter.

embedding.summary: California Medical Political Action Committee (CALPAC) seeks FPPC guidance on whether Form 420 campaign statements must include a treasurer's residential address and phone number, arguing that the committee's business address and phone suffice and that residential information raises privacy concerns.
